messages.content:
Customer Profile
Customer: Itaú Unibanco
Department: Customer Experience
Use Case Category: Operations/Analytics
Interest: Real User Monitoring & Mobile Experience
Pain Points

Difficulty in defining "satisfactory performance": Each application and business has a unique definition of satisfactory performance, making it challenging to establish universal benchmarks based on Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID/INP, CLS).
Lack of visibility into specific user interactions: It's hard to track and map specific business interactions beyond page navigation, such as button clicks, form submissions, or custom user actions.
Inability to connect frontend navigation with backend requests: Understanding the complete user journey from frontend actions through to backend API processing via distributed tracing is a challenge.
Difficulty in identifying drop-off points in the user journey: Without clear visualization of aggregated user journeys and session timelines, it's hard to pinpoint where users abandon processes.
Limited insight into performance bottlenecks: Difficulty understanding which phase of page load (DNS, TCP connection, request/response, DOM processing) is causing slowness.

Key Metrics
Core Web Vitals & User-Centric Metrics

Largest Contentful Paint (LCP): Measures when the largest content element becomes visible (target: <2.5s for good experience)
Interaction to Next Paint (INP) / First Input Delay (FID): Quantifies responsiveness to user interactions (target: INP <200ms, FID <100ms)
Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS): Measures visual stability and unexpected layout shifts (target: <0.1)
First Contentful Paint (FCP): Time until first content is rendered
Total Blocking Time (TBT): Sum of blocking time for long tasks during page load

Navigation Timing Breakdown

DNS Resolution Time: Duration of DNS query (domainLookupEnd - domainLookupStart)
TCP Connection Time: Time to establish connection including TLS (connectEnd - connectStart)
Time to First Byte (TTFB): Server response latency (responseStart - requestStart)
Response Download Time: Content download duration (responseEnd - responseStart)
DOM Processing Time: HTML parsing and rendering (domComplete - domLoading)
Load Event Duration: Time for load event execution (loadEventEnd - loadEventStart)

User Behavior & Session Data

Session ID: Groups all user actions into a session for chronological timeline analysis
Transaction Types: Categorizes interactions (page-load, route-change, user-interaction)
User Interaction Actions: Custom tracking of business events like "Clicked Transfer Funds" or "Submitted PIX Payment Form"
Resource Timing: Load time of JavaScript, CSS, images, and API calls (via Resource Timing API)

Error & Performance Indicators

JavaScript Errors: Runtime errors captured with stack traces
Long Tasks: Tasks blocking the UI thread >50ms
Slow API Calls: HTTP requests tracked via distributed tracing
Failed Resources: 4xx/5xx HTTP responses for assets or API calls

Distributed Tracing

Trace ID & Span ID: Connects frontend RUM transactions with backend APM traces
traceparent Header: W3C standard for end-to-end distributed tracing
Service Maps: Visualizes request flow from browser through microservices

Use Cases
1. Core Web Vitals Monitoring & Alerting
Define performance thresholds based on Google's Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP/FID, CLS) and create dashboards to track these metrics by:

Application/page type (e.g., account dashboard, payment flow, investment portal)
Device type (mobile vs desktop)
Geographic region
Network connection type (4G, 5G, WiFi)

Implementation: Use ES|QL queries to aggregate transaction.marks data (FCP, LCP) and experience metrics (CLS, INP) across sessions, filtering by transaction.name and custom labels.
2. Mapping Customer Journeys with Custom Transactions
Instrument banking workflows using custom transactions and user timing marks:

"Login Flow": Capture login attempt → authentication → account dashboard
"PIX Transfer": Track form open → beneficiary selection → amount entry → confirmation → success
"Investment Purchase": Monitor product browse → details view → purchase → confirmation

Implementation: Use the RUM Agent's custom transaction API and data-transaction-name attributes to create named transactions. Track funnel progression with ES|QL queries joining session.id across transaction sequences.
3. Individual User Session Analysis
Filter by specific session ID in Kibana to view a detailed chronological timeline showing:

All page loads and route changes
User interaction events (clicks, form submissions)
API calls with response times (via distributed tracing)
JavaScript errors with stack traces
Long tasks that blocked the UI
Resource loading waterfall

Implementation: Query transactions and spans filtered by session.id, visualized in APM UI timeline or custom Kibana dashboards.
4. Building Aggregated Conversion Funnels
Create funnel visualizations to identify drop-off points:

Account opening: Homepage → Registration → Verification → First Deposit
Loan application: Calculator → Application Form → Document Upload → Approval
Card activation: Dashboard → Card Settings → Activation → Success

Implementation: Use Kibana Lens funnel charts or ES|QL queries to count unique sessions progressing through transaction.name sequences, grouped by custom labels indicating funnel steps.
5. Frontend-to-Backend Performance Correlation
Connect browser performance with backend service latency using distributed tracing:

Identify which API endpoints contribute most to slow page experiences
Trace specific user sessions from browser click → API gateway → microservices
Correlate frontend LCP/TBT metrics with backend transaction duration

Implementation: Configure distributedTracingOrigins in RUM Agent to propagate traceparent headers. Query APM data joining RUM transactions (via trace.id) with backend spans to visualize end-to-end latency.
6. Navigation Timing Breakdown Analysis
Diagnose which phase of page load causes performance issues:

DNS/TCP issues indicating network problems
High TTFB pointing to slow backend services
Long Response times suggesting large payloads or slow downloads
Extended DOM Processing indicating heavy JavaScript execution or rendering

Implementation: Use breakdown metrics in ES|QL queries (span.type: DNS, TCP, Request, Response, Processing, Load) to identify bottlenecks by percentile (p50, p95, p99).
7. Long Task & JavaScript Error Correlation
Identify patterns between long-running JavaScript tasks and user-reported issues:

Track long tasks that block interactions during critical workflows
Correlate JavaScript errors with specific user journeys
Monitor third-party script impact on performance

Implementation: Query span.type: "longtask" and error events, grouping by transaction.name, url.full, and user_agent details to identify problematic code paths.
8. Resource Loading Optimization
Analyze resource timing to optimize asset delivery:

Identify slow-loading assets (images, JS, CSS) by resource type
Track CDN performance and cache hit rates
Monitor third-party resource impact

Implementation: Query span.type: "resource" with breakdown by resource.url, http.response.status_code, and duration to identify optimization opportunities.

Excellent! I've captured comprehensive details for Itaú Unibanco's Customer Experience team - 5 pain points around RUM & mobile experience, 8 detailed use cases covering Core Web Vitals monitoring through resource optimization, and 18 key metrics. To finalize the demo, I just need:

• **Scale:** How many daily active users, sessions, or page views are you monitoring?

💡 Don't have all the details? Just type **'skip'** and I'll use reasonable defaults.

Perfect! I've filled in reasonable defaults:

• **Scale:** 100+ team members managing 1,000+ transactions daily
• **Metrics:** Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) - target <0.1, Total Blocking Time (TBT), DNS Resolution Time, Long Tasks (>50ms), Load Event Duration, Response Download Time, Session ID, Slow API Calls, Time to First Byte (TTFB), DOM Processing Time, Trace ID & Span ID, First Contentful Paint (FCP), First Input Delay (FID) - target <100ms, TCP Connection Time, JavaScript Errors, Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) - target <2.5s, Interaction to Next Paint (INP) - target <200ms, Failed Resources (4xx/5xx)

You're all set! Type **'generate'** to create your custom demo module.

pain_points: Inability to connect frontend navigation with backend requests through distributed tracing
Difficulty in defining 'satisfactory performance' - each application and business has unique performance definitions, challenging to establish universal benchmarks based on Core Web Vitals
Limited insight into performance bottlenecks across different phases of page load (DNS, TCP, request/response, DOM processing)
Lack of visibility into specific user interactions beyond page navigation (button clicks, form submissions, custom user actions)
Difficulty in identifying drop-off points in the user journey without clear visualization of aggregated user journeys and session timelines
use_cases: Individual User Session Analysis - view detailed chronological timeline of page loads, user interactions, API calls, errors, and long tasks
Navigation Timing Breakdown Analysis - diagnose which phase of page load causes performance issues
Resource Loading Optimization - analyze resource timing to optimize asset delivery and CDN performance
Frontend-to-Backend Performance Correlation - connect browser performance with backend service latency using distributed tracing
Long Task & JavaScript Error Correlation - identify patterns between long-running tasks and user-reported issues
Building Aggregated Conversion Funnels - identify drop-off points in account opening, loan application, card activation flows
Core Web Vitals Monitoring & Alerting - track LCP, INP/FID, CLS by application type, device, region, and network connection
Mapping Customer Journeys with Custom Transactions - instrument banking workflows like Login Flow, PIX Transfer, Investment Purchase
